title: MCP Tool Reference
tagline: Every mcp__dbt_index__ tool, signatures and returns
summary: Complete reference for all seventeen mcp__dbt_index__* tools dbt Wizard exposes. Every call is a structured MCP tool call backed by a local DuckDB index of the dbt project (models, columns, tests, edges) plus read-only warehouse access, never a file read or grep. Tools fall into groups: project status and the project index, DAG-aware intelligence, warehouse integration, dbt-native CLI calls, business context, MetricFlow metrics, build timings, and dev-vs-prod diff. dbt CLI tools auto-refresh the index on success and support deferred state for cost-efficient builds. This page reflects dbt Wizard's own description of its capabilities; the signatures here are authoritative and the individual feature pages conform to this reference.
whyDifferent: A general AI coding agent answers questions by reading .sql and .yml files and grepping for strings, so "what breaks if I drop brand_name" becomes a text match against files that happen to mention the column. These tools query a live DuckDB index that already knows grain, primary keys, materializations, test pass/fail state, and DAG edges, then resolve {{ ref() }} and {{ source() }} against the warehouse. The result is column-level blast radius with severity ratings and live data previews instead of a list of files that contain a substring.

capabilities.title: Project status and index
capabilities.detail: status returns project shape (node counts by type and layer), doc and test coverage, and a last-run summary, all index-backed with no warehouse round-trip. describe, search, and changeset read the same local DuckDB index: describe gives full node introspection (columns, compiled SQL, tests, lineage, catalog types, grain, PK markers); search does BM25 metadata or regex-against-SQL with DAG context always attached; changeset does epoch-based diff since the last flush.

capabilities.title: DAG-aware intelligence
capabilities.detail: impact, lineage, and timings traverse the actual edge graph. impact returns severity-rated column- and node-level blast radius; lineage does depth-controlled upstream/downstream traversal scoped to a subgraph; timings surfaces slowest nodes and critical-path bottlenecks from per-node durations and OTel trace data captured into the index from the last run.

capabilities.title: Warehouse integration
capabilities.detail: warehouse is read-only by design: it issues SELECT-only statements against the warehouse (Snowflake, BigQuery, and others) for live previews with no dbt run, and rejects DML/DDL. dbt_show resolves full Jinja ({{ ref() }}, {{ source() }}, macros) and executes the compiled SQL against the warehouse, returning real rows so you can catch valid-but-wrong-data bugs that compiled SQL alone would not reveal.

capabilities.title: dbt-native CLI calls
capabilities.detail: dbt_build, dbt_run, dbt_compile, dbt_test, and dbt_parse are structured tool calls (not shell strings). They auto-refresh the index on success and support --defer --state for cost-efficient deferred dev builds.

capabilities.title: Business context
capabilities.detail: context resolves glossary, fiscal, org, and compliance business terms, mapping jargon like "annual revenue" to canonical dbt terms (ARR) and the models that implement them, with optional synonym resolution, linked-model expansion, and gap surfacing.

capabilities.title: Metrics, timings, and diff
capabilities.detail: metrics queries the MetricFlow semantic layer (list/describe/run/dry_run); timings exposes slowest nodes and the critical path; diff compares dev vs production state for safe PR validation, and requires either dbt Cloud or deferred state (--defer --state pointed at a production manifest/artifacts path).

examplePrompts.prompt: Give me the shape of this project: node counts, doc and test coverage, and the last run.
examplePrompts.intent: Index-backed project overview before diving in

examplePrompts.prompt: What breaks if I drop brand_name from stg_products?
examplePrompts.intent: Column-level blast radius before a destructive change

examplePrompts.prompt: Show me everything two hops downstream of fct_orders.
examplePrompts.intent: Scoped downstream lineage traversal with depth control

examplePrompts.prompt: What's the grain and PK of int_orders_enriched, and are its tests passing?
examplePrompts.intent: Full node introspection from the index

examplePrompts.prompt: Find every model that references the discount_pct column.
examplePrompts.intent: Regex-against-SQL search with DAG context always attached

examplePrompts.prompt: Preview the top 10 rows of dim_customers in the warehouse without running dbt.
Build int_orders_enriched and everything downstream using deferred prod state, then run its tests.
examplePrompts.intent: Cost-efficient deferred dev build plus tests

examplePrompts.prompt: What does our glossary mean by annual revenue and which model owns it, and what's the slowest node in the build?

interaction.scenario: An SE is demoing tool selection: they open with a project-shape question, then rapid-fire unrelated prompts at dbt Wizard and watch it route each to the correct mcp__dbt_index__* tool, ending with a deferred build and a dev-vs-prod diff via deferred state.
interaction.userPrompt: Give me the shape of this project, then tell me what breaks if I drop brand_name from stg_products.

events.body: Give me the shape of this project: node counts, doc and test coverage, and the last run.
This is a project-overview question, answered straight from the index with no warehouse round-trip. I'll call status.
66 nodes (24 staging, 11 intermediate, 9 marts, 4 exposures). 81% documented, 74% tested. Last run: 61 of 62 passed, 1 errored.
What breaks if I drop brand_name from stg_products?
This is a column-level blast-radius question, not a text search. I'll scope impact to the stg_products node and the brand_name column rather than grepping for the string.
3 downstream columns affected, 1 critical (fct_orders), 2 tests at risk.
Next prompt is a lineage question. Different tool: lineage with a hop limit, downstream only.
Now show me everything two hops downstream of fct_orders.
2 nodes at hop 1, 1 exposure at hop 2.
What's the grain and PK of int_orders_enriched, and are its tests passing?
Grain: one row per order_id. PK order_id. 7 tests pass, 1 fails (not_null on brand_name, 42 rows).

The failing not_null on brand_name lines up with the blast radius from earlier. The SE's last ask is to validate a fix safely, so I'll build the subtree with deferred prod state, then diff dev vs prod via that same state.
Build int_orders_enriched and downstream with deferred prod state, then diff against prod.

events.code:
PASS int_orders_enriched (12.4s)
PASS fct_orders (8.1s)
PASS mart_revenue_daily (3.2s)
PASS mart_brand_performance (2.9s)
4 of 4 models built OK, deferred refs resolved to target-prod/. Index refreshed.
{"node":"fct_orders","schema_changes":[],"row_changes":{"added":0,"removed":0,"modified":42},"note":"42 modified rows: brand_name now populated where prod had NULL"}

events.body: Routed each prompt to the right tool with no file reads or greps. status: 66 nodes, 81% documented, 74% tested, last run 61 of 62 passing. impact: dropping stg_products.brand_name hits 3 downstream columns, fct_orders critical, 2 tests at risk. lineage: fct_orders feeds mart_revenue_daily and mart_brand_performance, then the Looker exposure. describe: int_orders_enriched is one row per order_id keyed on order_id, with a failing not_null on brand_name (42 rows). The deferred dbt_build of int_orders_enriched+ resolved unselected refs to target-prod/ and passed all 4 models, and diff vs prod (same deferred state) shows exactly those 42 rows now populated with brand_name and zero schema drift. Safe to PR.

Post-edit validation subagent: re-ran the failing not_null on int_orders_enriched.brand_name after the build. 0 failing rows now; the 42-row diff matches the rows that flipped from NULL to populated. The change is consistent end to end.

seNotes: These signatures are authoritative and reflect dbt Wizard's own description of its 17 mcp__dbt_index__* tools. The individual feature pages conform to this reference; if a feature page and this page ever disagree, this page wins.

Open the demo with status: one call returns project shape (node counts by type and layer), doc and test coverage, and the last-run summary, all index-backed. It is the fastest way to show a prospect that the index already knows the project before you touch a single model.

Lead the rest of the demo with tool selection, not output: the point is the wizard picks impact vs lineage vs search correctly from plain English. "What breaks" routes to impact (severity), "show me downstream" routes to lineage (hops), "where is X referenced" routes to search.

Hammer the file-read contrast. Ask the room how a generic agent answers "what breaks if I drop brand_name" — it greps and returns files containing the string. impact returns severity-rated column-level blast radius from real DAG edges, including tests at risk.

Call out that the dbt CLI tools (dbt_build/run/compile/test/parse) are structured calls, not shell strings, and they auto-refresh the index on success — so status, describe, and impact stay current right after a build with no manual reindex.

Always demo --defer --state on dbt_build/dbt_run: pass state as a path to the production manifest/artifacts (represented here as target-prod/) so unselected refs resolve to prod and dev builds only materialize the selected subtree. This is the cost-efficiency talking point for large warehouses.

diff compares dev vs production and requires either dbt Cloud or deferred state (--defer --state pointed at the production manifest/artifacts path). Both are valid: do not claim dbt Cloud is mandatory, and do not claim deferred state is the only option. If a prospect has neither a dbt Cloud connection nor a produced prod state yet, show warehouse for a live read-only preview, then come back to diff once one of those is in place.

Supported warehouses for the warehouse and dbt_show tools include Snowflake, BigQuery, and others; confirm the exact supported list for the release the prospect is on before promising a specific platform.
timings is grounded in per-node durations, critical path, and OTel trace data captured from the last run into the index. Frame it as real measured durations from the last run, not schedule estimates.

context resolves glossary, fiscal, org, and compliance business terms; use the kind argument to scope the lookup. warehouse is read-only by design (parses the statement, allows SELECT only, rejects DML/DDL); dbt_show executes a model's compiled SQL and returns real rows, catching valid-but-wrong-data bugs that reading compiled SQL alone would miss. metrics results come from the same governed MetricFlow definition the dashboard uses, so a number from metrics reconciles with the dashboard rather than drifting like a hand-written GROUP BY; its where argument uses MetricFlow filter template syntax, e.g. {{ Dimension('customer__segment') }}, not a raw SQL predicate.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__status
tools.purpose: Index-backed project overview: project shape (node counts by type and layer), doc and test coverage, and a last-run summary, with no warehouse round-trip.

params.name: include
params.desc: Optional sections to return: node_counts, coverage, last_run.

tools.returns: Node counts by type/layer, documented and tested coverage percentages, and a last-run summary (status, models run, passed/errored, finish time), all read from the local index.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__impact
tools.purpose: DAG-aware blast radius for a node or specific column, with severity ratings, by traversing edges in the index rather than grepping references.

params.name: node
params.desc: Target model/node, e.g. stg_products.

params.name: column
params.desc: Optional column to scope blast radius to a single field, e.g. brand_name.

params.name: change_type
params.desc: Optional kind of change being assessed (e.g. drop, rename, type change) to refine severity.

params.name: max_depth
params.desc: Optional downstream hop limit for the traversal.

params.name: scope
params.desc: Optional subgraph filter (e.g. layer or path) to bound the report.

tools.returns: Ranked list of affected downstream nodes/columns each tagged severity critical/high/medium/low, with the edge path explaining why.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__lineage
tools.purpose: Upstream/downstream DAG traversal with hop-depth control, edge-type filtering, and optional column-level scoping.

params.name: node
params.desc: Starting node, e.g. fct_orders.

params.name: direction
params.desc: upstream, downstream, or both.

params.name: max_depth
params.desc: Number of hops to traverse.

params.name: edge_types
params.desc: Filter edges (e.g. ref vs source) included in traversal.

params.name: column
params.desc: Optional column to trace column-level lineage.

tools.returns: Ordered node list with hop distance and edge type per relationship.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__describe
tools.purpose: Full node introspection from the index: columns, compiled SQL, test results, lineage, catalog types, plus grain, PK markers, schema, and materialization.

params.name: node
params.desc: Node to introspect, e.g. int_orders_enriched.

params.name: include
params.desc: Optional sections to return: columns, compiled_sql, tests, lineage, catalog.

tools.returns: Structured node profile: column list with types, grain, PK markers, materialization, schema, last-run test pass/fail, and compiled SQL.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__search
tools.purpose: BM25 metadata search or regex against SQL, with DAG context always attached to every hit.

params.name: query
params.desc: Search terms or regex pattern.

params.name: mode
params.desc: bm25 (metadata) or regex (against SQL).

params.name: scope
params.desc: Optional subgraph/layer filter.

params.name: limit
params.desc: Optional cap on the number of matches returned.

tools.returns: Ranked matches, each with the node, the matched snippet, and its DAG context (upstream/downstream neighbors). DAG context is always attached; there is no separate flag to request it.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__changeset
tools.purpose: Epoch-based diff of what changed in the project since the last index flush, optionally annotated with downstream impact and filtered to a node set.

params.name: since_epoch
params.desc: Integer epoch marker to diff from; defaults to last flush.

params.name: with_impact
params.desc: Attach downstream impact analysis to each change.

params.name: node_filter
params.desc: Optional selector to limit the changeset to specific nodes.

tools.returns: List of added/modified/removed nodes and columns since the epoch, optionally with severity-rated downstream impact.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__warehouse
tools.purpose: Run live SQL directly against the warehouse (Snowflake, BigQuery, and others) for data previews without a dbt run. Read-only by design: the tool issues SELECT-only statements and rejects DML/DDL.

params.name: sql
params.desc: SELECT statement to execute; DML/DDL is rejected before it reaches the warehouse.

params.name: limit
params.desc: Optional row cap for the preview.

params.name: timeout_s
params.desc: Optional query timeout in seconds.

tools.returns: Result rows and column headers from the warehouse. Read-only by design: the statement is parsed and only SELECT is allowed, so DML/DDL is rejected rather than executed.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__dbt_show
tools.purpose: Execute a model's compiled SQL (full Jinja resolution of {{ ref() }}, {{ source() }}, macros) against the warehouse and return real preview rows, so you can catch valid-but-wrong-data bugs that compiled SQL alone would not reveal.

params.name: node
params.desc: Model selector to compile and run for a preview.

params.name: limit
params.desc: Optional preview row count.

params.name: state
params.desc: Optional deferred state path for resolving refs, e.g. target-prod/.

tools.returns: Compiled SQL plus the resulting preview rows after Jinja/macro expansion and execution against the warehouse.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__dbt_build
tools.purpose: Structured dbt build (run + test) as an MCP call; auto-refreshes the index on success and supports deferred state.

params.name: select
params.desc: dbt selector, e.g. int_orders_enriched+ for the node and downstream.

params.name: exclude
params.desc: Optional selector to exclude.

params.name: defer
params.desc: Defer unselected refs to a prior state for cost-efficient dev builds.

params.name: state
params.desc: Path to the deferred production manifest/artifacts, e.g. target-prod/.

tools.returns: Per-node run + test status (success/error/skipped) with row counts; triggers an index refresh on success.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__dbt_run
tools.purpose: Structured dbt run as an MCP call; auto-refreshes the index on success and supports deferred state.

params.name: select
params.desc: Models to run via dbt selector syntax.

params.name: exclude
params.desc: Optional selector to exclude.

params.name: defer
params.desc: Defer unselected refs to prior state.

params.name: state
params.desc: Path to the deferred production manifest/artifacts, e.g. target-prod/.

params.name: full_refresh
params.desc: Force full refresh of incremental models.

tools.returns: Per-model materialization status and timing; index refresh on success.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__dbt_compile
tools.purpose: Structured dbt compile as an MCP call; resolves Jinja to executable SQL without touching the warehouse.

params.name: select
params.desc: Models to compile via dbt selector syntax.

tools.returns: Compiled SQL per selected node; index refresh on success.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__dbt_test
tools.purpose: Structured dbt test as an MCP call; runs schema.yml and data tests and refreshes the index.

params.name: select
params.desc: Tests/models to test via selector syntax.

params.name: defer
params.desc: Defer unselected refs to prior state.

params.name: state
params.desc: Path to the deferred production manifest/artifacts, e.g. target-prod/.

tools.returns: Per-test pass/fail with failing row counts; index refresh on success.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__dbt_parse
tools.purpose: Structured dbt parse as an MCP call; rebuilds the manifest and validates project graph integrity.

params.name: (none)
params.desc: No required parameters; parses the whole project.

tools.returns: Parse status with any compilation/graph errors; refreshes the index on success.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__context
tools.purpose: Business-context resolution over glossary, fiscal, org, and compliance docs, mapping jargon to canonical dbt terms and the models that implement them.

params.name: query
params.desc: Business phrase to resolve, e.g. "annual revenue".

params.name: kind
params.desc: Optional domain filter: glossary, fiscal, org, or compliance.

params.name: resolve_synonyms
params.desc: Expand the query across known synonyms before matching.

params.name: include_linked_models
params.desc: Return the dbt models/columns linked to the resolved term.

params.name: surface_gaps
params.desc: Flag terms with no governed definition or no implementing model.

tools.returns: Canonical term (e.g. ARR), definition, synonyms, the dbt models/columns that implement it, and any flagged gaps.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__metrics
tools.signature: mcp__dbt_index__metrics(action, metric?, group_by?, where?, order_by?, limit?)
tools.purpose: MetricFlow semantic-layer interface: list, describe, run, and dry_run metrics against governed metric definitions.

params.name: action
params.desc: list, describe, run, or dry_run.

params.name: metric
params.desc: Metric name for describe/run/dry_run.

params.name: group_by
params.desc: Dimensions/time grains to group by on run.

params.name: where
params.desc: MetricFlow filter template syntax, e.g. {{ TimeDimension('metric_time','month') }} or {{ Dimension('customer__segment') }}, not a raw SQL column predicate.

params.name: order_by
params.desc: Optional ordering for run results.

params.name: limit
params.desc: Optional row cap on run results.

tools.returns: Metric catalog, metric definition, or query results (or generated SQL for dry_run). Results come from the same governed metric definition the dashboard uses, so they reconcile rather than drifting like a hand-written GROUP BY.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__timings
tools.purpose: Build performance analysis: slowest nodes and critical-path bottlenecks from per-node durations and OTel trace data captured from the last run into the index.

params.name: view
params.desc: slowest or critical_path.

params.name: node
params.desc: Optional node to scope timing detail to.

params.name: limit
params.desc: How many nodes to return for the slowest view.

tools.returns: Ranked node timings and/or critical-path nodes with per-node durations, sourced from the last run's results and OpenTelemetry (OTel) trace data as recorded in the index.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__diff
tools.signature: mcp__dbt_index__diff(node, state?, defer?, primary_key?, columns?, dimensions?)
tools.purpose: Compare dev vs production for a node for safe PR validation. Requires either dbt Cloud or deferred state (--defer --state pointed at a production manifest/artifacts path); both are valid.

params.name: node
params.desc: Node to diff, e.g. fct_orders.

params.name: state
params.desc: Path to the production manifest/artifacts to compare against (deferred-state mode), e.g. target-prod/.

params.name: defer
params.desc: Resolve unselected refs to the production state during the comparison (deferred-state mode).

params.name: primary_key
params.desc: Optional primary key to align rows for the comparison.

params.name: columns
params.desc: Optional subset of columns to compare.

params.name: dimensions
params.desc: Optional dimensions to aggregate the diff by.

tools.returns: Row-level and schema-level differences between the dev build and the production state for the node. Requires dbt Cloud or deferred state (--defer --state) as the production reference.
